description: Multi-mode (intraday / short_term / long_term) scoring engine for IN equities. Backward-compatible with v1.0.1 — all 36 rules, 5 overrides, IDs, and the universal scoring_formula are preserved verbatim. The 'modes' object lets the runtime select which pillars contribute to composite without rewriting rules; the 'schedule' sub-object inside each mode tells the API scheduler when to dispatch fresh runs.
_changelog: v2.0.1: Renamed mode keys swing→short_term, investing→long_term to match the Python `Mode` literal and the frontend `StrategyMode`. Added a 'schedule' object per mode and a top-level scheduler block under auto_mode_logic.markets.IN so apps/api/scheduler.py can load cadence + market hours from the same file.
v1.0.1: Sentiment pillar overhauled to news NLP + FII/DII + sector RS + price/volume spike + VIX contrarian. Earnings Guidance rule removed.

sentiment.description: News NLP, FII/DII flows, sector RS, price/volume spikes, India VIX contrarian

macro.description: Interest rates, GDP, inflation, sector rotation

risk.description: Beta (market-regime adjusted) and India VIX volatility risk

_pillar_weight_note: Risk pillar (0.12) now comprises Beta + India VIX volatility only; Earnings Guidance rule removed. Sentiment pillar (0.15) covers: news NLP score, FII/DII flows (IN), sector RS, intraday price+volume spike, and India VIX contrarian (IN). Sentiment and valuation (0.18) together carry 0.33 weight, unchanged from prior version. Overall composite formula unchanged.

scoring_formula.sentiment_intra_pillar: sentiment_score = weighted_mean(SENT-001..005 rule scores) using each rule's category_weight
scoring_formula.risk_intra_pillar: risk_score = weighted_mean(RISK-001 beta + RISK-003 vix_vol) each at category_weight=0.50
_change_notes: Sentiment: replaced analyst-ratings/insider/short-interest/institutional/news-NLP rules with news-NLP, FII-DII-flow, sector-RS, price-volume-spike, VIX-contrarian
Risk: removed RISK-002 (Earnings Guidance); redistributed its 0.40 weight equally to RISK-001 (0.50) and RISK-003 (0.50)
Top-level composite weights and thresholds unchanged

thresholds.action: Full position; consider leverage if risk-tolerant
thresholds.notes: All pillars aligned. Rare confluence signal.

thresholds.action: Initiate position; scale in on pullbacks
thresholds.notes: Most factors positive. Normal entry point.

thresholds.action: Hold existing; no new entry; tight stop-loss

intraday.description: 5-min polling between 09:15 and 15:30 IST. Only fast-reacting pillars matter; fundamentals/valuation/macro carry zero weight because they barely move within a session.

thresholds.notes: All intraday-active pillars confluent. Rare in a session.

thresholds.action: No new entry; manage existing only
thresholds.notes: Mixed intraday signals — wait for confirmation.

thresholds.action: Full exit; consider intraday short if mandate allows

intraday._rationale: Intraday holds < 1 session — quarterly EPS, P/E, GDP, FII flows have no causal link to a 5-minute move. Sector RS and price/volume spike (SENT-003/004) DO matter and are kept in the active sentiment pillar.

short_term.description: Daily run at 10:00 IST (Mon-Fri) for 1-10 day positional trades. Adds macro back in but still leaves out fundamentals/valuation — short-term trades are technical setups, not value buys.

thresholds.action: Hold existing; no new swing entries
thresholds.notes: Mixed signals; sit out the chop.

short_term._rationale: Short-term trades exit before the next earnings print — fundamental rerating risk dominates if you hold through. Macro + sector rotation (MACRO-004) is the actual edge for IN short-term books.

long_term.description: Long-term holding (months to years). The original universal model — every pillar contributes. Run once per session at 10:00 IST (Mon-Fri); the same schedule that drives short_term so the dashboard refreshes both buckets in lockstep.

thresholds.action: Full position; consider leverage if risk-tolerant
thresholds.notes: All pillars aligned. Rare confluence signal.

thresholds.action: Initiate position; scale in on pullbacks
thresholds.notes: Most factors positive. Normal entry point.

thresholds.action: Hold existing; no new entry; tight stop-loss

long_term._rationale: Holding period > 1 quarter — every pillar matters: earnings quality, balance sheet, valuation discipline, and macro regime determine total return. Same composite as v1.0.1.

auto_mode_logic.description: Default mode chosen automatically when caller does not specify one. IST-aware so the same orchestrator can be reused in US markets later by adding a 'US' market block.

IN.user_override: Caller may force any mode via API param ?mode=intraday|short_term|long_term — auto logic is a default, never a hard rule.

US._note: Reserved for future US-market support. When enabled, set timezone='America/New_York', trading_hours 09:30-16:00 ET, and reuse the same intraday/short_term/long_term modes since pillar mechanics are market-agnostic.

confidence_normalization.description: How the engine normalises composite_score so inactive pillars never depress confidence.
confidence_normalization.implementation: RuleEvaluator._composite_score iterates rules, multiplies score by (mode.category_weights[rule.category] * rule.category_weight * rule.confidence_weight), and divides by the sum of those weights. Rules in inactive pillars (weight == 0) drop out of BOTH numerator and denominator — the 0..100 range is preserved across modes.
confidence_normalization.formula: composite = sum(rule.score * w for evaluated rules with w > 0) / sum(w for evaluated rules with w > 0)
confidence_normalization.skipped_rules_handling: Skipped rules (missing data) contribute nothing to either sum. Their absence is reported via data_coverage_pct, not via score depression.
confidence_normalization._invariant: An intraday STRONG_BUY (composite 78/100) is directly comparable to an investing STRONG_BUY (composite 78/100) — both are normalised against their mode's active pillar set.

rules.name: EPS Growth (YoY & QoQ)
rules.description: Earnings per share growth measures profitability expansion.
rules.rationale: Earnings per share growing >10% YoY = strong BUY signal. Consecutive EPS misses for 2+ quarters = SELL signal. Look for acceleration in growth rate.

rules.name: Revenue Growth Rate
rules.description: Top-line growth signals demand health.
rules.rationale: Consistent revenue growth >8% signals healthy demand. Flat or declining revenue for 2 quarters raises concern.

rules.name: Net Profit Margin
rules.description: Operational efficiency relative to industry.
rules.rationale: Expanding margins = operational efficiency. Margin compression >3pp over 2 quarters = red flag.

rules.name: Return on Equity (ROE) & ROA
rules.description: Capital efficiency vs sector peers.
rules.rationale: ROE >15% with consistent trend signals strong capital efficiency. ROE rising without leverage increase = quality signal.

rules.name: Free Cash Flow (FCF)
rules.description: Cash generation after capex - the gold standard.
rules.rationale: Positive and growing FCF is the gold standard. FCF yield >5% = undervalued. Negative FCF for 3+ quarters in a mature company = strong SELL.

rules.name: Debt-to-Equity (D/E) Ratio
rules.description: Financial leverage; sector-adjusted.

financials._note: Banks use different metric; deprioritize this rule

rules.rationale: D/E <1.0 for most sectors = healthy. D/E >3.0 = risky. Trend matters: rising D/E = warning.

rules.name: Current Ratio & Quick Ratio
rules.description: Short-term liquidity strength.
rules.rationale: Current ratio >1.5 indicates sufficient short-term liquidity. Below 1.0 = potential liquidity crisis warning.

rules.name: Interest Coverage Ratio
rules.description: EBIT / Interest Expense - default risk indicator.
rules.rationale: EBIT / Interest Expense >3x = comfortable. Below 1.5x means earnings barely cover interest — high default risk.

rules.name: 50-Day vs 200-Day MA (Golden/Death Cross)
rules.description: Trend confirmation via MA crossover.
rules.rationale: Golden Cross (50MA crosses above 200MA) = bullish trend confirmation BUY. Death Cross = bearish trend SELL signal.

rules.name: Price vs 52-Week Range
rules.description: Position within annual price range.
rules.rationale: Trading near 52-week high with strong fundamentals = momentum BUY. Near 52-week low with no fundamental reason = possible value trap or SELL.

rules.name: EMA Ribbon (8/13/21/34/55)
rules.description: Multi-timeframe EMA alignment for trend strength.

conditions.strong_buy: ema_8 > ema_13 AND ema_13 > ema_21 AND ema_21 > ema_34 AND ema_34 > ema_55 AND ribbon_compression_pct > 5
conditions.buy: ema_8 > ema_13 AND ema_13 > ema_21 AND ema_21 > ema_34
conditions.sell: ema_8 < ema_13 AND ema_13 < ema_21 AND ema_21 < ema_34
conditions.strong_sell: ema_8 < ema_13 AND ema_13 < ema_21 AND ema_21 < ema_34 AND ema_34 < ema_55

rules.rationale: All EMAs aligned and fanning upward = strong uptrend BUY. Ribbon compression followed by downward cross = impending reversal SELL signal.

rules.name: RSI (Relative Strength Index, 14-period)
rules.description: Overbought/oversold and divergence detection.
rules.rationale: RSI <30 = oversold, potential BUY reversal. RSI >70 = overbought, potential SELL/correction. Divergence = strong SELL warning.

rules.name: MACD (12, 26, 9 - Signal Line Crossover)
rules.description: Trend-following momentum oscillator.

conditions.strong_buy: macd_line > macd_signal AND macd_histogram > 0 AND macd_histogram > macd_histogram_prev
conditions.strong_sell: macd_line < macd_signal AND macd_histogram < 0 AND macd_histogram < macd_histogram_prev

rules.rationale: MACD line crossing above signal line = BUY. Crossing below = SELL. Histogram rising from zero = bullish momentum building.

rules.name: Stochastic Oscillator (14, 3, 3)
rules.description: Range-bound momentum indicator.

conditions.strong_buy: stoch_k < 20 AND stoch_d < 20 AND stoch_k > stoch_d AND stoch_k_prev <= stoch_d_prev
conditions.strong_sell: stoch_k > 80 AND stoch_d > 80 AND stoch_k < stoch_d AND stoch_k_prev >= stoch_d_prev

rules.rationale: %K crossing %D from below in <20 zone = BUY. Crossing from above in >80 zone = SELL.

rules.name: Volume Confirmation
rules.description: Volume validates price moves.

conditions.strong_buy: is_breakout == true AND volume_today >= (volume_avg_20d * 2.0) AND price_change_today_pct > 0

rules.rationale: Price breakout + above-average volume = HIGH confidence BUY. Heavy sell volume on down days = distribution SELL signal.

rules.name: Bollinger Bands (20, 2)
rules.description: Volatility-adjusted price extremes.
rules.rationale: Price touching lower band + RSI <30 = high-probability BUY setup. Price at upper band + negative divergence = SELL.

rules.name: Support & Resistance Levels
rules.description: Key price zones from historical clusters and Fibonacci.
rules.rationale: Bounce off major support with volume = BUY. Breakdown below key support level with volume = confirmed SELL.

rules.name: News Sentiment (NLP Score, 14-day)
rules.description: 14-day rolling average tone of financial news articles from GDELT, normalized to [-1, +1]. Captures media narrative shift before price action.
rules.rationale: Strongly positive news tone (>=0.5) signals broad media bullishness — BUY. A spike in negative-volume articles is a leading SELL indicator. News sentiment leads institutional repositioning by 1-3 days.

rules.name: FII/DII Net Flow Sentiment
rules.description: Net daily FII (Foreign Institutional Investor) and DII (Domestic Institutional Investor) cash flows into Indian equities as a smart-money sentiment gauge. FII flow dominates; DII flow acts as counterbalancing signal.
rules._note_market_scope: FII/DII flow data is specific to Indian markets (NSE reporting). Rule disabled for US.
rules.rationale: FII net buy >=₹3000 Cr with DII support = strong institutional conviction BUY. Heavy FII outflow <=-₹3000 Cr with DII also selling = panic-exit STRONG SELL. DII buying alone when FII sells = mild support, not a full BUY.

rules.name: Sector Relative Strength (3-Month)
rules.description: Stock's 3-month return relative to its sector index. Measures whether the market is rotating into or out of this stock compared to sector peers — a leading sentiment indicator.
rules.rationale: Outperforming the sector by 10%+ over 3 months signals institutional accumulation and positive relative sentiment. Underperforming the sector by 10%+ = distribution and negative sentiment regardless of absolute returns.

rules.name: Intraday Price & Volume Spike
rules.description: Detects unusual single-day price moves accompanied by volume spikes — a real-time sentiment shock indicator. Captures both positive catalysts (earnings beats, upgrades) and negative shocks (fraud alerts, downgrades).
rules.rationale: A 5%+ up move on 2.5x average volume = clear positive sentiment shock (earnings beat, major upgrade). A 5%+ down move on equal volume = negative shock (guidance cut, scandal). Volume must confirm — price moves on thin volume are noise.

rules.name: India VIX Sentiment (Contrarian)
rules.description: India VIX (NSE fear gauge) used as a contrarian sentiment signal. Extreme fear (high VIX) can signal capitulation BUY opportunities; extreme complacency (low VIX with rising VIX trend) signals hidden risk.
rules._note_market_scope: India VIX (NSE) is specific to the Indian equity market. Rule disabled for US.
rules.rationale: India VIX >=30 and falling = fear peaked + reverting = contrarian BUY. VIX <15 but spiking upward = complacency being unwound = SELL warning. VIX >=35 and spiking fast = panic cascade in progress = STRONG SELL (risk management exit, not contrarian buy).

rules.name: Interest Rate Environment
rules.description: Central bank policy regime affects sector preference.
rules.rationale: Falling rates = BUY for growth/tech/REIT. Rising rates = BUY for financials, SELL for high-duration bonds and unprofitable growth stocks.

rules.name: GDP Growth & Economic Cycle
rules.description: Macro regime determines cyclical vs defensive preference.
rules.rationale: Expansion phase: BUY cyclicals, industrials, tech. Contraction: BUY defensives. Recession signals = broad SELL alert on cyclicals.

rules.name: Inflation Rate (CPI/PPI)
rules.description: Inflation regime affects sector preference and stagflation risk.
rules.rationale: Moderate inflation (2-3%): neutral. High inflation: BUY commodities, energy, materials. Stagflation = broad SELL signal.

rules.name: Sector Rotation & Relative Strength
rules.description: Stock vs sector vs market 3-month performance.

conditions.strong_buy: rs_line_at_3m_high == true AND stock_return_3m_pct > sector_return_3m_pct AND stock_return_3m_pct > market_return_3m_pct

rules.rationale: Stock outperforming its sector and the broad market = BUY. Persistent sector underperformance over 3 months = SELL or avoid.

rules.name: Currency & Geopolitical Risk
rules.description: FX impact on multinationals + geopolitical exposure.

conditions.buy: (market == 'IN' AND inr_change_3m_pct_IN < 0 AND foreign_revenue_pct >= 30) OR (market == 'US' AND dxy_change_3m_pct_US < -3)
conditions.sell: (market == 'US' AND dxy_change_3m_pct_US > 5 AND foreign_revenue_pct >= 40) OR geopolitical_exposure_score >= 0.5

rules.rationale: Strong USD hurts US multinational revenue (SELL risk). Geopolitical escalation in key market regions = SELL for exposed stocks.

rules.name: FII Net Flow Signal
rules.description: Foreign Institutional Investor net cash flow into Indian equities (₹ crore) as a macro liquidity signal.
rules.rationale: FII net buying >₹2000 cr = strong macro inflow BUY. Heavy FII outflow <-₹2000 cr = macro SELL warning. FIIs are the dominant marginal buyer in Indian large-caps.

rules.name: Put-Call Ratio Sentiment
rules.description: NSE option-chain Put-Call Ratio as a contrarian sentiment gauge.
rules.rationale: PCR >1.2 = excessive put-buying / hedged market = contrarian BUY. PCR <0.8 = call-side froth, overleveraged longs, vulnerable to unwind = SELL.

rules.name: P/E Ratio (vs Sector & Historical)
rules.description: Price-to-earnings against sector average and own history.

conditions.strong_buy: pe_ratio < (sector_pe_avg * 0.7) AND forward_pe < pe_ratio AND revenue_growing == true
conditions.sell: pe_ratio > sector_pe_avg * 1.5 OR (pe_ratio > 50 AND is_growth_stock == false)

rules.rationale: P/E <15 and below sector average = value BUY. P/E >50 for non-growth company = overvalued SELL.

rules.name: PEG Ratio (Price/Earnings to Growth)
rules.description: P/E adjusted for earnings growth rate.
rules.rationale: PEG <1.0 = undervalued relative to growth (BUY). PEG >2.0 = growth fully priced in (SELL).

rules.name: EV/EBITDA
rules.description: Capital structure-neutral valuation.
rules.rationale: EV/EBITDA <10x for mature companies = attractive. Useful for capital-intensive and leveraged firms.

rules.name: Price-to-Book (P/B) Ratio
rules.description: Market price vs accounting book value.

conditions.strong_buy: pb_ratio < 1.0 AND roe_pct >= 10 AND is_financial_sector == true

rules.rationale: P/B <1.0 may indicate deep value or distress. Best for banks and financials.

rules.name: Dividend Yield & Payout Ratio
rules.description: Income sustainability and dividend safety.
rules.rationale: Dividend yield >3% with payout ratio <60% = sustainable income BUY. Payout ratio >90% with declining earnings = dividend cut risk SELL.

rules.name: Beta (Market Sensitivity)
rules.description: Stock volatility relative to market — regime-dependent. High beta amplifies both gains and losses; interpretation depends on whether the market is in a bull or bear regime.
rules._market_regime_note: market_regime is derived from MACRO-002 (GDP) and MACRO-001 (rates) — bull = expansion+cuts, bear = contraction+hikes
rules.rationale: Beta <1.0 = defensive; prefer in bear markets. Beta >1.5 = amplifies market moves; suits bull market BUY. High-beta stocks get SELL signal first in downturns.

rules.name: India VIX (NSE) Market Volatility Risk
rules.description: India VIX (NSE) fear gauge used as a pure risk-management signal — distinct from the contrarian sentiment use in SENT-005. Focuses on position-sizing and stop-loss triggers.
rules.rationale: India VIX (NSE) >30 = fear/capitulation zone — contrarian BUY for strong fundamentals. India VIX <15 = complacency, reduce position sizes. Spiking India VIX (5d change >=50%) = immediate risk management SELL trigger.

override_rules.name: Immediate SELL — Fraud / Accounting Restatement
override_rules.trigger: has_fraud_allegation == true OR has_material_restatement == true OR has_sec_sebi_investigation == true
override_rules.rationale: Any credible allegation of accounting fraud, material restatement, or SEC/SEBI investigation = IMMEDIATE SELL regardless of confidence score.

override_rules.name: SELL — Guidance Withdrawn Without Explanation
override_rules.rationale: Company withdraws full-year guidance with no clear explanation mid-year = high uncertainty SELL. Management visibility has collapsed.

override_rules.name: SELL — Dividend Cut on Declining Earnings
override_rules.rationale: A company cutting its dividend while earnings are declining signals cash flow stress and broken investment thesis.

override_rules.name: BUY Pause — Stop-Loss Triggered
override_rules.rationale: If price falls >8% below purchase price, pause all BUY signals and reassess. Prevent averaging down into a broken thesis without fresh fundamental review.

override_rules.name: Concentration Risk Cap
override_rules.rationale: No single stock should exceed 10% of total portfolio regardless of confidence score.
